pose-vs-remplissage.answerHeading: Nail extensions vs fill: which service do you need?
pose-vs-remplissage.answerBlock: A full set of nail extensions is your starting point when your natural nails are short or when you want to begin wearing gel or acrylic nails. A fill — also called a refill — is the maintenance appointment you book every three to four weeks to keep an existing set looking fresh.
pose-vs-remplissage.body: At Ongles Maily in Carrefour Beauport, our technicians perform full sets and fills every day. Knowing the difference helps you plan your visits and manage your beauty budget. A full set involves applying gel or acrylic over your natural nails, usually with a tip extension to add length. You choose your shape — square, almond, oval, or coffin — and your technician sculpts, seals, and finishes with your chosen colour or nail art. Expect the appointment to take between 90 and 120 minutes depending on the complexity of the design. A fill is your maintenance visit. As your natural nail grows, a gap appears at the base of your existing set. Your technician fills that gap with fresh product, re-shapes the curve of each nail, and refreshes the colour. A fill typically takes 60 to 90 minutes. If you wait longer than five weeks between appointments, the gap becomes too wide and your technician may recommend removing the old set entirely and starting fresh — leaving it too long increases the risk of lifting and can trap moisture under the product. Our advice: if you are new to nail extensions, book a full set. If your existing set is intact and less than five weeks old, a fill is the right choice. Our team will assess your nails on arrival and recommend the best approach. Walk-ins are welcome at Carrefour Beauport.
pose-vs-remplissage.metaTitle: Nail extensions vs fill: which is right for you? | Ongles Maily Beauport
pose-vs-remplissage.metaDescription: Full set or fill at Ongles Maily? Learn the differences, timing, and what our technicians recommend at Carrefour Beauport, Quebec.

manucure-vs-pedicure.answerHeading: Manicure vs pedicure: what is the difference?
manucure-vs-pedicure.answerBlock: A manicure focuses on your hands and fingernails, while a pedicure is dedicated to your feet and toenails. Both services include skin care, nail shaping, and your choice of polish — the difference is simply which part of the body is treated.
manucure-vs-pedicure.body: At Ongles Maily, many clients combine a manicure and a pedicure in a single visit for complete care from fingertip to toe. Here is everything you need to know to choose between them — or book both. A manicure begins with a warm water soak to soften the skin around your nails. Your technician gently pushes back or removes cuticles, then files and shapes your nails to your preferred shape — square, round, almond, or stiletto. A nourishing lotion is applied and a hand massage follows before the polish is applied. At Ongles Maily, all tools are sterilized after each client. A pedicure follows a similar flow but adds an extra step: exfoliation and a heel scrub to remove calluses that build up from daily walking. The extended foot soak is deeply relaxing and relieves tired, aching feet. Your technician then applies a moisturizing balm, performs a foot and lower-leg massage, and finishes with nail polish. If you need to choose just one service, think about which nails are most visible in your daily life. If you work with your hands or wear rings, prioritize a manicure. If you love open-toe shoes or suffer from dry heels, a pedicure will give you immediate comfort and visible results. For the best results, most of our clients schedule a manicure every two weeks and a pedicure every three to four weeks.
manucure-vs-pedicure.metaTitle: Manicure vs pedicure: complete comparison | Ongles Maily Beauport
manucure-vs-pedicure.metaDescription: Manicure or pedicure at Ongles Maily? Compare the two services and find out which one — or both — suits your routine at Carrefour Beauport.

gel-vs-acrylique.answerHeading: Gel vs acrylic nails: which is right for you?
gel-vs-acrylique.answerBlock: At Ongles Maily, we offer both gel and acrylic — two professional nail systems that deliver lasting results. Gel cures under a UV or LED lamp for a natural, glossy finish, while acrylic is air-dried and renowned for its exceptional strength and durability.
gel-vs-acrylique.body: Gel and acrylic are the two leading technologies for long-lasting nail enhancements at professional salons. At Ongles Maily in Carrefour Beauport, our technicians are trained in both systems and will help you choose the one that best fits your lifestyle. Gel nails are applied as a liquid or soft putty that hardens under a UV or LED lamp. The finish is smooth, slightly flexible, and very glossy. Gel colours are vibrant and chip-resistant. Because the product is flexible, gel tends to feel more comfortable for clients who prefer nails that move a little with the natural nail. Gel requires a fill approximately every three weeks. Acrylic is created by mixing a liquid monomer with a powder polymer, forming a paste that hardens as it is shaped. It is slightly firmer than gel once set, making it an excellent choice for clients with naturally brittle nails or those who want longer extensions. Acrylic withstands everyday impacts well — typing, light gardening, cooking, and sports. It soaks off easily when it is time for removal and also needs a fill every three to four weeks. Both products are used daily across our salons and meet the strictest hygiene standards. All tools are replaced or sterilized between clients. Whether you choose gel or acrylic, you will leave with beautiful, durable nails. We recommend a consultation on your first visit so our technician can examine your natural nails and make the most suitable recommendation for you.
gel-vs-acrylique.metaTitle: Gel vs acrylic nails: the complete guide | Ongles Maily Beauport
gel-vs-acrylique.metaDescription: Gel or acrylic at Ongles Maily? Compare durability, finish, and maintenance to find your perfect nail system at Carrefour Beauport, Quebec.

meilleur-pour.answerHeading: Which nail service is best for you?
meilleur-pour.answerBlock: The best nail service depends on your lifestyle: for maximum durability, gel or acrylic extensions are unbeatable and last three to four weeks. For everyday nail maintenance, a classic manicure keeps your hands looking polished. For special occasions — weddings, events, celebrations — a full set with custom nail art is what we recommend.
meilleur-pour.body: Choosing the right nail service can feel overwhelming when there are so many great options. At Ongles Maily, we see clients with every kind of need, from quick lunch-break manicures to elaborate wedding designs. Here is a practical guide to help you find the best fit. For durability in daily life, gel or acrylic nail extensions are the clear winner. Both systems hold up for three to four weeks without chipping, even with an active lifestyle. If you spend a lot of time at a keyboard, in the garden, or using your hands for manual work, acrylic is especially resistant to impact. Gel offers a more natural, flexible finish that many clients prefer for a polished office look. For special occasions — weddings, proms, galas, photo shoots, or any event where your hands will be noticed — a full set with custom nail art is worth the investment. Our technicians create bespoke designs: floral patterns, ombre gradients, crystals, and delicate freehand art. Allow 90 minutes to two hours for a full art appointment. For regular nail upkeep on short natural nails, a classic manicure every two weeks keeps cuticles neat, skin hydrated, and colour fresh. A pedicure every three weeks keeps your feet smooth and your toenails looking great even in sandals. If you are pregnant, have sensitive skin, or are concerned about product ingredients, let our team know. We offer formaldehyde-free polishes and gentler application techniques suited to every client. Walk-ins welcome — no appointment necessary.
meilleur-pour.metaTitle: Which nail service is best for you? | Ongles Maily Beauport
meilleur-pour.metaDescription: Manicure, pedicure, gel or acrylic? Find out which nail service fits your lifestyle best at Ongles Maily, Carrefour Beauport, Quebec.

nearMe.answerHeading: Nail salon in Beauport — Ongles Maily at Carrefour Beauport
nearMe.answerBlock: Ongles Maily is Beauport's go-to nail salon, located at Carrefour Beauport, 3333 rue du Carrefour, right off the Félix-Leclerc Expressway and the Robert-Bourassa interchange. Beauport is a residential borough on the eastern side of Québec City, and our salon has served its residents for over 15 years — welcoming clients from Giffard, Montmorency, and the surrounding east-end neighbourhoods. Free parking is available directly at the shopping centre, making it easy to fit an appointment into your day. Whether you live in Beauport or are passing through, you will find a full menu of nail services at Ongles Maily: full nail enhancements, fills, manicures, and pedicures. Our hygiene standards set us apart — every tool is disinfected between clients and every workstation is sanitized after each service. You can book your appointment online at any time or simply walk in; both options are always welcome. We are open seven days a week to fit around your schedule, with no need to plan far in advance.
nearMe.body: Carrefour Beauport sits at the corner of rue du Carrefour and boulevard Raymond, easily reachable from Route 138 and Highway 440. The salon is located near entrances 4 and 5. Free on-site parking.
